title: NOC 73201 – General building maintenance workers and building superintendents | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 73201 professionals are the operational heartbeat of Canada's real estate and institutional sectors. As a TEER 3 occupation, these workers ensure that residential complexes, office towers, hospitals, and schools remain safe, functional, and efficient. In 2026, as Canada integrates "Smart Building" automation and green energy retrofits, these professionals are classified as Essential Infrastructure Managers. For immigration, this role is a prime target for the 2026 OINP In-Demand Skills Stream and the Trade Occupations Category in Express Entry.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level3Major Group73 – General tradesSub-Major Group732 – Building maintenance installers, servicers and repairersMinor Group7320 – Building maintenance installers, servicers and repairersNOC Code73201

duties: System Adjustments: Make adjustments and minor repairs to heating, cooling, ventilating, plumbing, and electrical systems (HVAC).
Minor Repairs: Perform routine maintenance tasks such as painting, drywall repair, and replacing faulty light fixtures or switches.
Trade Coordination: Identify major mechanical or structural failures and contact specialized tradespersons (electricians, plumbers) for major repairs.
Preventive Inspection: Conduct daily and weekly inspections of building systems to ensure compliance with safety and fire codes.
Inventory Management: Maintain an inventory of repair supplies and equipment and make minor purchases as required for building upkeep.
Resident Response: Respond promptly to requests from tenants or residents for minor in-suite or office repairs.
Security Oversight: Ensure that security and safety measures, including fire alarms and surveillance systems, are fully operational.
Site Preparation: Coordinate the cleaning and preparation of housing units or office spaces prior to the arrival of new residents/tenants.
Supervision: (Superintendents) Supervise other maintenance staff or cleaning crews in large residential or commercial establishments.
Administrative Duties: May advertise vacancies, show apartments to prospective tenants, and collect rent or security deposits.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Experience: Previous maintenance experience is often a mandatory requirement for senior superintendent roles.

Certifications:A Building Operator Certificate (Level 1 or 2) is highly valued in 2026.A journeyman/woman certificate in one or more trades (e.g., Electrical or Plumbing) may be required for large-scale industrial complexes.A valid driver’s license is often required for workers managing multiple sites.
A Building Operator Certificate (Level 1 or 2) is highly valued in 2026.
A journeyman/woman certificate in one or more trades (e.g., Electrical or Plumbing) may be required for large-scale industrial complexes.
A valid driver’s license is often required for workers managing multiple sites.
Security: A criminal record check (Bondable) is standard, as workers have access to private residential and commercial spaces.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, experienced Building Superintendents in major urban hubs like Toronto and Vancouver often earn annual salaries between $58,000 and $82,000, frequently including a subsidized or free on-site apartment.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. With nearly 37% of the current maintenance workforce aged 55 and older, a massive "Retirement Wave" will create over 30,000 job openings across Canada over the next decade. As the "Build-to-Rent" sector expands, the demand for professionalized building management will continue to grow through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for building maintenance workers in 2026 is Strong, fueled by a record-high volume of new multi-unit residential buildings (MURBs) and a shift toward professionally managed rental housing. In Ontario, British Columbia, and Alberta, the outlook is "Good" due to the aging of existing building stock requiring constant repair. According to March 2026 data, the industry is facing a "Skills Gap" in workers capable of managing computerized Building Management Systems (BMS). Approximately 82% of workers in this NOC receive at least one type of non-wage benefit, reflecting a stable and competitive employment market.
The "Smart Building" Revolution: Gain experience managing high-tech HVAC systems, automated security, and IoT-enabled energy monitoring.
Stable Public & Private Roles: Employment opportunities range from global management firms like BGIS and Dexterra to municipal school boards and health authorities.
Diverse Skill Integration: This role provides a broad foundation in plumbing, electrical, and carpentry, often serving as a bridge to specialized Red Seal trades.
Strategic PR Pathways: This NOC was explicitly targeted in March 2026 OINP draws, proving its status as a high-priority "In-Demand" occupation for provincial nomination.
pathways: Ontario (OINP) In-Demand Skills Stream: Frequently targets NOC 73201 for regional draws (e.g., Central and Eastern Ontario).
Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Included in category-based selection for those with 6 months of experience in a relevant trade role.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): For maintenance workers with 2 years of experience and a valid job offer or certificate of qualification.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-conversion route for those with a job offer from a designated building management company in the Atlantic provinces.
